overThePastTwoDecadesCapitalHasMovedFrom: Over the past two decades, capital has moved from public to private markets. Private assets have grown tenfold since the early 2000s, now exceeding $6 trillion globally.
privateEquityVentureCapitalAndCryptoOnce: Private equity, venture capital, and crypto — once for the ultra-wealthy — are now open to all. YASBe breaks down traditional barriers, inviting everyday investors to take part.

thisTypeOfInvestmentGivesYouAPotentialCl: This type of investment gives you a potential claim to equity (or a cash equivalent) at a later time. Honeybee uses a structure called the Crowd SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity). It's important to note that SAFE doesn't mean the investment is low-risk—it simply refers to the legal framework used.
digitalAssetsAreBlockchainBasedTokensOrC: Digital assets are blockchain-based tokens or cryptocurrencies that may increase in value depending on market demand and the utility of the asset. You might encounter agreements like a Token DPA (an interest-bearing loan repaid in tokens or cash) or a Token Purchase Agreement (TPA), which is a contract promising token delivery in the future—it's not an equity, debt, or the token itself.
debtInvestingMeansYoureLendingMoneyToACo: Debt investing means you're lending money to a company with the expectation of repayment plus interest. Honeybee offers Crowd Term Notes, which specify fixed interest rates and scheduled repayment terms based on the loan amount.
withRevenueSharingModelsYouEarnAPortionO: With revenue-sharing models, you earn a portion of the company’s revenue without owning any equity. This is typically structured through Crowd Revenue Notes, where repayments are based on a percentage of the issuer’s income until a certain cap is reached.
whatYouGetWhenYouInvest: What you get when you invest?
whenYouInvestThroughYASBeYouProvideCapit: When you invest through YASBe, you provide capital in exchange for a financial interest. This is governed by a formal agreement with the fundraising entity.
fromLumpSumPayoutsToOngoingEarningsEachI: From lump sum payouts to ongoing earnings—each investment is structured differently, with over $25M already returned to investors.
someOfferAOneTimePayoutOthersProvideOngo: Some offer a one-time payout. Others provide ongoing, recurring payments depending on the deal structure.
acrossStartupsCryptoRealEstateAndMoreWev: Across startups, crypto, real estate, and more—we've facilitated over USD 25 million in investor returns.
everyCampaignHasTwoKeyFundingBenchmarks: Every campaign has two key funding benchmarks:
theLowestAmountTheStartupMustRaiseToSucc: The lowest amount the startup must raise to succeed. If they don't hit this target by the campaign's end, the deal falls through, and all investments are refunded.
theHighestAmountTheStartupIsWillingToAcc: The highest amount the startup is willing to accept. Once reached, the campaign stops taking new investments.
startupsSetTheirOwnMinimumAndMaximumInve: Startups set their own minimum and maximum investment limits:
theLowestAllowedIsTypicallyUSD100000: The lowest allowed is typically USD100,000
someCompaniesCapMaximumInvestmentsToEnsu: Some companies cap maximum investments to ensure broader participation.
eachFundraisingCampaignRunsForAFixedPeri: Each fundraising campaign runs for a fixed period, whether or not the startup meets its funding goals. Here’s what you should know:
theseAreSetInAdvanceAndDontChangeInvesto: These are set in advance and don’t change. Investors can commit funds anytime before the deadline, even if the funding goal is already met.
ifAStartupHitsItsMinimumGoalEarlyItMayCh: If a startup hits its minimum goal early, it may choose to shorten or extend the campaign under certain conditions.
someStartupsMayKeepTheCampaignOpenAfterH: Some startups may keep the campaign open after hitting their minimum goal while receiving funds incrementally. Investors will be notified if this happens.
48HoursBeforeTheCampaignEndsOrAfterARoll: 48 hours before the campaign ends (or after a rolling close is triggered), investments become final and non-refundable.
ifAStartupAdjustsItsTermsAfterLaunchInve: If a startup adjusts its terms after launch, investors must reconfirm their commitment.
youllHave14CalendarDaysToRespondToAnyCha: You’ll have 14 Calendar Days to respond to any changes.
ifYouDontReconfirmYourInvestmentIsAutoma: If you don’t reconfirm, your investment is automatically canceled and refunded.
anyFutureContributionsWillBeTreatedAsANe: Any future contributions will be treated as a new investment.
whenStartupsRaiseCapitalOnYASBeTheyDefin: When startups raise capital on YASBe, they define their own deal terms. Here's a breakdown of how it all works

beforeYouInvestTakeTimeToUnderstandTheRi: Before you invest, take time to understand the risks, rewards, and potential. This quick checklist will help
privateInvestmentsCarryRiskEspeciallySta: Private investments carry risk, especially startups. Never invest more than you can afford to lose.
ifItIsTooComplexToExplainItMayNotBeTheRi: If it's too complex to explain, it may not be the right fit. Focus on what you understand or learn more in our Investor Academy.
lookIntoThePeopleBehindTheDeal: Look Into the People Behind the Deal
aGreatIdeaNeedsGreatExecutionResearchThe: A great idea needs great execution. Research the founder's experience and track record.
takeTimeToReadThroughTheDetailsAndThenRe: Take time to read through the details - and then read them again. Some things to focus on:
differentRegulationsLikeRegCFRegAOrRegSC: Different regulations (like Reg CF, Reg A+, or Reg S) come with different terms. Look into minimum investments, funding targets, and perks or discounts.
whatMilestonesHasTheCompanyHitSoFarWhats: What milestones has the company hit so far? What’s their plan to reach the next stage?
theCommentsSectionCanBeAGoldmineItsWhere: The comments section can be a goldmine. It’s where founders interact with potential investors — and where you can ask questions or learn from others.
ratherThanGoingAllInOnASingleOpportunity: Rather than going all-in on a single opportunity, consider making several smaller investments. It not only lowers your risk but also helps you gain broader experience.
isSomeoneWithExperienceBackingTheSameDea: Is someone with experience backing the same deal, under the same terms? If so, why are they in — and what do they see in the opportunity?
beyondFinancialReturnThinkAboutTheBigger: Beyond financial return, think about the bigger picture. Ask yourself how this investment aligns with your values, and whether it contributes to a future you want to help build.
